hero.question: Israel strike on Yemen by March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd has essentially ruled out an Israeli strike on Yemen by year-end 2026 at 15%, pricing in either regional de-escalation or continued restraint despite Houthi provocations. Watch for U.S. pressure shifts and whether Iran's nuclear negotiations break down, which would reshape Israeli calculus on proxy threats and make this 15% look like free money on the YES side.

daily_take.headline: Yemen Strike Odds Jump 4 Points. Market's Finally Pricing Reality.
daily_take.deck: Israel strike on Yemen jumped from 11% to 15% in 24 hours on $586K volume, signaling fresh conviction from sharp money. The market was criminally underpriced when you consider Israel's track record of regional strikes, Yemen's continued Houthi threat, and Netanyahu's political need for wins before March 2026. The real question isn't if, but when. This move is overdue, not overdone.

sidebar.headline: US-Iran entry odds barely budged while Israel-Yemen surged. Smart money clearly doesn't see direct American escalation as the next domino.
sidebar.label: US Forces Enter Iran: 5.2%

sidebar.headline: Arizona's tournament odds spiked 2.2 points on thin volume. Check if this reflects real betting or noise from sharps testing the market depth.
sidebar.label: Arizona NCAA Title: 34.4%

sidebar.headline: DHS funding odds climbing as March approaches. Low volume market but shows Congress is finally moving from theater to actual legislation.
sidebar.label: DHS Funded by April 15: 21.5%
